nyheter.title: 10-ship Russian Convoy Transits Japan’s Tsushima Strait
nyheter.summary: A 10-ship Russian convoy transited Japan’s Tsushima Strait to enter the East China Sea over the weekend, according to Japan’s Joint Staff Office. The Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force (JMSDF) spotted two Russian corvettes, a fleet oiler, a tugboat and six cargo ships around 5 p.m. Saturday sailing southwest 140 kilometers northeast of Tsushima, and from Saturday to Sunday, the convoy transited the strait, reads a Monday news release from Japan’s Joint Staff Office. nyheter.title: 2 Dead, 1 Survivor in Friday Strike on Suspected Narco Boat
nyheter.summary: U.S. forces struck a suspected narco boat Friday, killing two men and leaving one survivor, U.S. Southern Command announced. The boat was operating in the Eastern Pacific and is alleged to have been transiting along known narco-trafficking routes when it was struck. Following the strike, SOUTHCOM notified the U.S. Coast Guard of the survivor. The Coast Guard had not announced an update in the search and rescue of the survivor as of early Monday. nyheter.title: Future Aircraft Carrier Doris Miller Delayed by 2 Years
nyheter.summary: One of the Navy’s Ford-class aircraft carriers is delayed by two years, stretching the schedule to build the future USS Doris Miller (CVN-81) out to 15 years, USNI News has learned. Carrier Doris Miller will now deliver to the Navy in February of 2034, according to the service’s Fiscal Year 2027 budget justification books. Last year’s books projected the ship’s delivery in February of 2032. “The CVN-81 delivery date shifted from February 2032 to February 2034 due to shipbuilder construction foot

nyheter.title: Strait of Hormuz Shipping in State of Confusion as Industry Watches U.S., Iranian Actions
nyheter.summary: Three Navy destroyers came under fire from Iran as they transited the Strait of Hormuz as commercial shipping traffic remains at an almost standstill. Iranian forces sent missiles, drones and small boats after USS Mason (DDG-87), USS Rafael Peralta (DDG-115) and USS Truxton (DDG-103) as the three warships headed toward the Gulf of Oman, according to U.S. Central Command news release. nyheter.title: GAO Report on Mariner Training
nyheter.summary: Why GAO Did This Study The maritime industry relies on mariners to work on vessels carrying goods and passengers domestically and internationally. However, industry stakeholders have raised concerns about a mariner shortage. To work in the industry, mariners often take courses through a training institution. nyheter.summary: The French Carrier Strike Group transited the Suez Canal on Wednesday en route to the Red Sea, as part of a multinational naval coalition that seeks to restore freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. “The multinational mission that France and the United Kingdom have set up can help restore confidence among shipowners and insurers. nyheter.title: Cahill Nominated to Lead Naval Surface Force, Cash Nominated as Navy’s Top Chaplain
nyheter.summary: President Donald Trump nominated Rear Adm. Joe Cahill to serve as the next commander of Naval Surface Force, the Pentagon announced Tuesday. Cahill, who is the current commander of Naval Surface Force Atlantic in Norfolk, Va., will be promoted to vice admiral if confirmed as the next SWO Boss. A career surface warfare officer, Cahill has served on several destroyers, Ticonderoga-class guided-missile cruiser USS Bunker Hill (CG-52) and Kitty Hawk-class aircraft carrier USS Constellation (CV-64),

nyheter.title: Carrier USS Gerald R. Ford is Now in the Atlantic
nyheter.summary: Aircraft carrier USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78) is now operating in the Atlantic Ocean after sailing through the Strait of Gibraltar on Wednesday, according to ship spotters. As of Wednesday, Ford has been deployed for 315 days, the longest carrier strike group deployment since the end of the Cold War. Since the start of the Vietnam War, only USS Coral Sea (CVA-43) in 1964 and USS Midway (CVA-41) in 1972 have deployed longer for national tasking. Ford, the Navy’s newest carrier, was initially

nyheter.title: ‘Littoral Deep Battle’
nyheter.summary: LAOAG, Philippines — Target drones resembling Chinese amphibious infantry fighting vehicles approached the La Paz Sand Dunes at a steady pace. In the next few hours, a combined force of American, Philippine, Canadian and Japanese troops took turns tearing into the simulated beach landing, trialing a plan devised by the U.S. Army to defeat amphibious invasions. nyheter.title: U.S. Tests Using Drones to Track, Target Suspected Narco Boats
nyheter.summary: U.S. 4th Fleet and U.S. Naval Southern Command recently tested the use of drones and artificial intelligence to track and target suspected narco boats and counter transnational organized crime. The drill, conducted in the waters off Key West, Fla., was part of this year’s iteration of the Navy’s annual Fleet Experimentation (FLEX) exercise, which spanned April 24-30, according to a Navy news release.
